channel.title: New local problems on FixMyStreet
channel.description: The latest local problems reported by users

item.title: Rubbish bags, 11th July
item.description:
Good morning, We have been experiencing a serious problem over the past few weeks. Every few days, a large number of rubbish bags are being dumped opposite my house. Earlier this week, your team cleared several bags, but more have already been dumped again. I opened one of the bags and found evidence identifying who is responsible. I had already suspected who it was because I have seen them dumping rubbish before. We also have video footage, although it is not clear enough to identify them beyond doubt. The people I believe are responsible live at 25 Alford Road. There appear to be a large number of people living at this address. I do not feel comfortable approaching them myself. I have already reported this issue to Nicola Taylor, who advised me to contact you so that it can be investigated. I would be grateful if you could investigate this matter and take the appropriate action to stop this ongoing fly-tipping. It is becoming a significant problem for our neighbourhood. Thank you for your time and assistance. I look forward to hearing from you.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Church Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Graffiti on hoarding., 11th July
item.description:
As above. Large graffiti denoting what?

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): 4-62, Weighton Road, Anerley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Van parks illegally and removes plates, 11th July
item.description:
Van has been parking illegally for last 4 months, and removing it's plates when left parking.

Nearest address to the pin placed on the map (from OS Places API): Brighton Road, Hackney, London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Tree rotting at side., 11th July
item.description:
As above. Tree has been marked for attention but trunk is breaking at an alarming rate.

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Croydon Road, Penge, Greater London

item.title: Road cracked and water gushing through, 11th July
The whole safety crossing area is smashed, 11th July
item.description:
Has been like this for at least 3 weeks, no reaction from anyone; please fix as it is used by many people throughout the day and also because it may fall on the road

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Chislehurst Road, Bickley, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Flytipped rubbish, keeps being added to, 11th July
item.description:
This pile of bricks etc has been there for at least 3 month; keeps increasing; NO street cleaning has been conducted during this time so this hasn't been pick up. please remove

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Logs Hill, Elmstead, Greater London

item.category: Fly-tip Large - More than a van load

item.title: Parking on the pavement in Epping high street. The audacity., 11th July
item.description:
This car drove straight onto the actual pavement and parked outside Costa for no apparent reason whatsoever.

Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Black bags full of household waste, 11th July
item.description:
Good morning, We have been experiencing a serious problem over the past few weeks. Every few days, a large number of rubbish bags are being dumped opposite my house. Earlier this week, your team cleared several bags, but more have already been dumped again. I opened one of the bags and found evidence identifying who is responsible and I replace the evidence in a blue council bag . I had already suspected who it was because I have seen them dumping rubbish before. We also have video footage, although it is not clear enough to identify them beyond doubt. The people I believe are responsible live at 25 Alford Road. There appear to be a large number of people living at this address. I do not feel comfortable approaching them myself. I have already reported this issue to Nicola Taylor, who advised me to contact you so that it can be investigated. I would be grateful if you could investigate this matter and take the appropriate action to stop this ongoing fly-tipping. It is becoming a significant problem for our neighbourhood. Thank you for your time and assistance. I look forward to hearing from you. Kind regards
Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Church Road, Northumberland Heath, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet

item.title: Camping in the rock gardens, Danson Park, 11th July
item.description:
Seen early this morning 11/7/26 -Someone (or more than one) is camping in the Rock gardens at Danson Park what 3 words location - wished.milk.smooth It is not the camping I object to, it is where they go to the toilet - human excrement is not nice to come across when you are out walking!!!

Nearest road to the pin placed on the map (automatically generated by OpenStreetMap): Danson Park, Welling, Greater London
Report on FixMyStreet
